what_it_does: This file is a self-contained interactive mobile information-architecture mockup for a 390×844 phone frame. It renders four gated full-screen views—Board, Talk, Queue, and Tune—controlled by a bottom tab bar, using inline CSS and HTML. It demonstrates a proposed mobile UI with warm-gold theming, state-coloured board nodes, a push-to-talk orb, a queue of approval cards, and a Tune screen that surfaces latent configuration and self-growth capabilities.
observations: Single-file HTML/CSS prototype with no visible external JavaScript in the shown portion.
Four-tab mobile IA: Board (◈), Talk (◇), Queue (▤), Tune (⚙).
Views are gated with display:none / .active, explicitly avoiding stacked transform sheets.
Warm-gold design system using CSS custom properties and a radial aurora background.
Board shows nodes coloured by state: idle, ran, cached, stuck, failed, live.
Floating thumb cluster on Board: Run button and + FAB for adding nodes.
Palette sheet and inspector sheet rise from the bottom when triggered.
Spotlight overlay for a 'show me' walkthrough on the clean board.
Talk view centers a breathing orb, conversation thread, offer card, F1 feedback chips, and push-to-talk button.
Queue view lists cards tinted by lane: decision, build-review, question.
Tune view consolidates settings, knobs, run-stats, self-grow actions, voice trial, and a self-change ledger.
data-ui-ref attributes annotate UI elements throughout the markup.
Extensive HTML comments document purpose, design decisions, and anti-patterns being avoided.
standouts: Explicit anti-S1 rationale: one focused view at a time via display:none, not a held transform sheet.
Status-as-light vocabulary with six distinct node-state colours and a legend.
Surfacing of latent capabilities in each tab, especially Tune (self-grow, /api/knobs, R2 setters, voice trial, ledger).
Push-to-talk orb as the dominant control in the Talk view.
Offer card for build/panel/extend self-modification surfaced in the conversation thread.
Warm-gold aurora, grain, dot grid, and breathing animations creating a calm-alive aesthetic.
conventions: CSS custom properties map to a design-token palette.
BEM-style class names and semantic section tags for each view.
data-* attributes used for UI references and node metadata.
Mobile-first fixed device frame with safe-area-like padding.
Inline documentation comments explaining design intent.
Google Fonts loaded via preconnect and stylesheet link.
concerns: File is truncated mid-markup, so the complete interactive behaviour and any scripts are not visible.
Very large inline stylesheet may be hard to maintain and audit.
Hardcoded 390×844 device dimensions may not represent other phones.
Limited accessibility markers (no visible aria-labels, focus indicators, or reduced-motion handling).
Heavy use of blur, gradients, and animations could affect low-end device performance.
External font dependency could break the intended look offline.
notes: A dense, design-rationale-heavy mobile mockup that tries to make a new tab-gated IA feel drivable and to surface every latent capability in its proper home.
questions: Where is the JavaScript that drives tab switching, the orb states, the Run button, and the spotlight steps?
Is this file intended to run standalone, or is it generated from / paired with IA-PROPOSAL.md and capability-map.md?
How are node states updated in a real implementation?
What exactly is the S1 bug being referenced, and where is it documented?
How do the data-ui-ref attributes map to actual components or tests?
Is the truncated remainder of the file available elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comments state it is a target mockup to realize the proposed mobile IA so it can be driven before the real build, and the markup indeed builds that interactive four-tab prototype.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to sell a new mobile IA architecture by making it tangible: a drivable prototype that fixes an earlier stacking bug, exposes buried features, and aligns with an established warm-gold design system, while documenting every design decision in the code itself.
novelty: The mockup embeds a full design argument inside HTML comments, uses a strict one-view-at-a-time gate as an explicit architectural fix, and maps a large set of latent backend capabilities onto recognisable mobile UI sections with state-colour and lane-tint languages.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, annotated mobile-IA prototype that encodes the four-tab gating pattern, a node-state colour vocabulary, and a layout for surfacing latent capabilities, which a fused system could keep as a reference implementation and design-rationale artifact.
summary_for_embedding: company/design/mockups/IA-mobile.html is a warm-gold, four-tab interactive mobile-IA HTML/CSS mockup for a 390×844 phone frame, demonstrating Board, Talk, Queue, and Tune views with state-coloured nodes, push-to-talk, and surfaced latent capabilities.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created as a drivable target mockup of a proposed mobile information architecture so stakeholders could experience the new tab-gated, status-as-light design before the real build.
